title: CsvFactory
description: AssetFactory implementation that loads CSV (and TSV) files and produces a two-dimensional array of strings. Parsing is RFC 4180-compliant: quoted fields (including fields that contain the delimiter, newlines, or double-quotes) are handled correctly. Line endings are normalised before parsing; empty trailing lines are dropped. The returned `string[][]` is intentionally untyped — callers decide whether to treat the first row as headers, apply type coercions, etc.

sections.title: Import
paragraphs: AssetFactory implementation that loads CSV (and TSV) files and produces a two-dimensional array of strings.

Parsing is RFC 4180-compliant: quoted fields (including fields that contain the delimiter, newlines, or double-quotes) are handled correctly. Line endings are normalised before parsing; empty trailing lines are dropped.
The returned `string[][]` is intentionally untyped — callers decide whether to treat the first row as headers, apply type coercions, etc.

members.name: create
members.description: Parses the CSV text into a two-dimensional array of strings. Each element of the outer array is one row; each element of an inner array is one field value with surrounding quotes stripped and escaped double-quotes ("") unescaped.

members.name: createObjectUrl
members.description: Creates an object URL from blob and registers it for automatic cleanup when destroy is called. Prefer this over calling URL.createObjectURL directly inside a factory subclass so the URL is always tracked.

members.name: destroy
members.description: Revokes every object URL that was created via createObjectUrl and clears the internal tracking pool.

members.name: process
members.description: Reads the response body as a UTF-8 string.

members.name: storageName
members.description: Identifier used as the object-store / storage-namespace key when this factory's assets are persisted to a CacheStore.
